records.description: Judgment of 27 August 1952.

records.title: ICJ judgment in Whaling in the Antarctic (Australia v. Japan: New Zealand intervening)
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Whaling in the Antarctic (Australia v.
records.description: Judgment of 31 March 2014.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 31 March 2014.
records.summary: Judgment of 31 March 2014. On 31 May 2010, Australia instituted proceedings against Japan in respect of “Japan’s continued pursuit of a large‑scale program of whaling under the Second Phase of its Japanese Whale Research Program under Special Permit in the Antarctic (‘JARPA II’), in breach of obligations assumed by Japan under the International Convention for the Regulation of Whaling (‘ICRW’), as well as its other international obligations for the preservation of marine mammals and the marine environment”.

records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued an advisory opinion in Conditions of Admission of a State to Membership in the United Nations (Article 4 of the Charter) on May 28, 1948.
records.description: Advisory Opinion of 28 May 1948. From the creation of the United Nations some 12 States had unsuccessfully applied for admission.
records.short_summary: Advisory Opinion of 28 May 1948. From the creation of the United Nations some 12 States had unsuccessfully applied for admission.
records.summary: Advisory Opinion of 28 May 1948. From the creation of the United Nations some 12 States had unsuccessfully applied for admission. Their applications were rejected by the Security Council in consequence of a veto imposed by one or other of the States which are permanent members of the Council. A proposal was then made for the admission of all the candidates at the same time. The General Assembly referred the question to the Court. In the interpretation it gave of Article 4 of the Charter of the United Nations, in it.

records.title: ICJ judgment in Fisheries Jurisdiction (Federal Republic of Germany v. Iceland)
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Fisheries Jurisdiction (Federal Republic of Germany v.
records.description: Judgment of 25 July 1974.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 25 July 1974.
records.summary: Judgment of 25 July 1974. On 14 April and 5 June 1972, respectively, the United Kingdom and the Federal Republic of Germany instituted proceedings against Iceland concerning a dispute over the proposed extension by Iceland, as from 1 September 1972, of the limits of its exclusive fisheries jurisdiction from a distance of 12 to a distance of 50 nautical miles. Iceland declared that the Court lacked jurisdiction, and declined to be represented in the proceedings or file pleadings. At the request of the United Kingdom.

records.description: Judgment of 19 December 1978. On 10 August 1976, Greece instituted proceedings against Turkey in a dispute over the Aegean Sea continental shelf.

records.summary: Judgment of 19 December 1978. On 10 August 1976, Greece instituted proceedings against Turkey in a dispute over the Aegean Sea continental shelf. It asked the Court in particular to declare that the Greek islands in the area were entitled to their lawful portion of continental shelf and to delimit the respective parts of that shelf appertaining to Greece and Turkey. records.title: ICJ judgment in Delimitation of the Maritime Boundary in the Gulf of Maine Area
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Delimitation of the Maritime Boundary in the Gulf of Maine Area (Canada/United States of America) on October 12, 1984.
records.description: Judgment of 12 October 1984.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 12 October 1984.
records.summary: Judgment of 12 October 1984. On 25 November 1981, Canada and the United States notified to the Court a Special Agreement whereby they referred to a Chamber of the Court the question of the delimitation of the maritime boundary dividing the continental shelf and fisheries zones of the two Parties in the Gulf of Maine area. This Chamber was constituted by an Order of 20 January 1982, and it was the first time that a case had been heard by an ad hoc Chamber of the Court.

records.title: ICJ judgment in Continental Shelf (Libyan Arab Jamahiriya/Malta)
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Continental Shelf (Libyan Arab Jamahiriya/Malta) on June 3, 1985.
records.description: Judgment of 3 June 1985. This case, which was submitted to the Court in 1982 by Special Agreement between Libya and Malta, related to the delimitation of the areas of continental shelf appertaining to each of these two States.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 3 June 1985. This case, which was submitted to the Court in 1982 by Special Agreement between Libya and Malta, related to the delimitation of the areas of continental shelf appertaining to each of these two States.
records.summary: Judgment of 3 June 1985. This case, which was submitted to the Court in 1982 by Special Agreement between Libya and Malta, related to the delimitation of the areas of continental shelf appertaining to each of these two States. In support of its argument, Libya relied on the principle of natural prolongation and the concept of proportionality. Malta maintained that States’ rights over areas of continental shelf were now governed by the concept of distance from the coast, which was held to confer a primacy on the equ.

records.title: ICJ judgment in Land, Island and Maritime Frontier Dispute (El Salvador/Honduras
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Land, Island and Maritime Frontier Dispute (El Salvador/Honduras: Nicaragua intervening) on September 11, 1992.
records.description: Judgment of 11 September 1992.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 11 September 1992.
records.summary: Judgment of 11 September 1992. On 11 December 1986, El Salvador and Honduras notified to the Court a Special Agreement whereby the Parties requested the Court to form a Chamber - consisting of three Members of the Court and two judges ad hoc - in order to (1) delimit the frontier line in the six sectors not delimited by the 1980 General Treaty of Peace concluded between the two States in 1980 and (2) determine the legal situation of the islands in the Gulf of Fonseca and the maritime spaces within and outside it. T.

records.title: ICJ judgment in Frontier Dispute (Benin/Niger)
records.one_liner: The International Court of Justice issued a judgment in Frontier Dispute (Benin/Niger) on July 12, 2005.
records.description: Judgment of 12 July 2005.
records.short_summary: Judgment of 12 July 2005.
records.summary: Judgment of 12 July 2005. On 3 May 2002, Benin and Niger, by joint notification of a Special Agreement signed on 15 June 2001 at Cotonou and which entered into force on 11 April 2002, seised the Court of a dispute concerning “the definitive delimitation of the whole boundary between them”. Under the terms of Article 1 of that Special Agreement, the Parties agreed to submit their frontier dispute to a Chamber of the Court, formed pursuant to Article 26, paragraph 2, of the Statute, and each to choose a judge ad hoc.
